content.claim: Early tracking in mathematics shows statistically inconsistent effects on mean national performance across studies, with some evidence of lower mathematics performance under early tracking significant only at the 10% level in one of three comparisons.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Statistical significance thresholds vary across underlying studies
Subject heterogeneity (mathematics versus reading) suggests effect is not uniform
Mean-performance findings are weaker and less consistent than inequality findings
Meta-analysis does not isolate Hungary's specific system or 6/8-year track design
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
